content: AUD assessment tools: (1) Screening—AUDIT (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test, 10 items), AUDIT-C (3-item consumption screen), CAGE questionnaire (Cut down, Annoyed, Guilty, Eye-opener), single-question screens ('How many times...'); (2) Diagnostic interviews—DSM-5 criteria assessment, SCID (Structured Clinical Interview for DSM); (3) Severity measures—AUDIT total score (8-15 hazardous, 16-19 harmful, 20+ dependent), DSM-5 severity specifiers; (4) Biomarkers—PEth (phosphatidylethanol) for heavy drinking past 2-4 weeks, GGT, MCV, CDT (carbohydrate-deficient transferrin), ethyl glucuronide in urine/hair; (5) Withdrawal risk—CIWA-Ar (Clinical Institute Withdrawal Assessment) for monitoring detoxification. Co-occurring mental health disorders common (depression, anxiety, PTSD) requiring integrated treatment. Liver function tests assess alcohol-related organ damage.
